availability.summary: Live probes returned 200 for fiscal-year trend, undocumented group alias, and subaward spending-level requests; invalid group values returned 400 with the live accepted value set.

sources.title: Structured reviewer report for RT1 map-plus-trend scope alignment

parameters.description: Time bucket for the returned series. Documentation lists canonical names; live/source validation also accepts short aliases and normalizes them in the response.

documented.notes: The staged contract omits short aliases.

observed.defaultBehavior: Alias 'fy' is accepted and response group is canonicalized to 'fiscal_year'.
observed.notes: Invalid values return HTTP 400 with the live enum set.

constraints: Use canonical names in generated requests unless reproducing live UI behavior that emits aliases.

parameters.description: Advanced search filter object scoping the award, transaction, or subaward population used for the time series.

documented.notes: The contract says filters is required and then defines many nested filter structures.

constraints: Live service returns an error when filters is absent.

parameters.description: Optional date ranges used to bound the spending population before aggregation.

documented.notes: Date semantics differ for transaction and subaward searches.

observed.notes: The live response includes a message that search dates are limited to an earliest date of 2007-10-01.

constraints: Dates are strings in YYYY-MM-DD form.
Use download endpoints for pre-2007 history.

parameters.description: Optional domestic-or-foreign place-of-performance scope filter. Set it explicitly when aligning a trend with place-of-performance geography outputs, especially state/county/district maps whose geography route documents a domestic default.

documented.notes: The staged spending_over_time contract lists this as an AdvancedFilterObject member.

observed.notes: Reviewer-reported validation and live execution accepted domestic and materially changed the monthly series relative to omitting the field.

constraints: Use this when the analytical scope is place of performance or when matching v2__search__spending_by_geography with scope=place_of_performance.
For same-scope state/county/district geography comparisons, set it explicitly instead of relying on the geography endpoint's domestic default.

parameters.description: Optional domestic-or-foreign recipient-location scope filter. Use it to align this trend with recipient-location geography summaries.

documented.notes: The staged spending_over_time contract lists this as an AdvancedFilterObject member.

observed.notes: Not separately live-probed in this bundle; exposed because the shared AdvancedFilterObject documents it and geography comparisons depend on deliberate scope alignment.

constraints: Use this when matching v2__search__spending_by_geography with scope=recipient_location.
For same-scope state/county/district recipient geography comparisons, set it explicitly instead of relying on the geography endpoint's domestic default.

parameters.description: Optional award type filter, commonly used to isolate contracts, grants, loans, IDVs, direct payments, or other assistance.
constraints: Use USAspending award type codes, not business labels.

parameters.description: Optional text search filter applied before aggregation.
constraints: Keyword searches can produce broad time series and should usually be combined with date or award-type filters.

parameters.description: Optional filter by program activity name or code. Source validation requires at least one member in each object.

documented.notes: The contract says at least one ProgramActivityObject member must be provided.

constraints: Each object should include name or code.

parameters.description: Legacy boolean that asks for subaward grouping instead of prime-award/transaction spending.

observed.defaultBehavior: Live responses now warn that the subawards field will be deprecated; set spending_level='subawards' instead.

parameters.description: Controls whether the series is calculated from transactions, award summaries, or subawards.
constraints: Subaward responses contain fewer award-category columns than transaction/award responses.

templates.name: contract_obligations_by_fiscal_year
templates.description: Two-fiscal-year contract obligation trend for a bounded search period.

templates.name: monthly_domestic_place_of_performance_trend_for_state_map_alignment
templates.description: Monthly contract trend explicitly scoped to domestic place of performance so it can be compared to a state/county/district place-of-performance map.

templates.name: subaward_quarter_series
templates.description: Quarterly subaward trend using the modern spending_level field.

response.shapeSummary: A time-series object with canonical group, resolved spending_level, ascending results rows, and warning messages. Each result row is one time bucket with aggregate amount plus category obligation/outlay columns; month and quarter buckets are encoded as fiscal periods inside time_period.

fields.description: Canonical group name used in the result.

observed.notes: Alias request group 'fy' is returned as 'fiscal_year'.

fields.description: Resolved spending level used to calculate the series.

Ascending time buckets; each bucket contains time_period, aggregated_amount, and category obligation/outlay columns. For month and quarter groupings, time_period is encoded in fiscal terms rather than calendar month or quarter numbers.

observed.notes: Transaction/award rows include Direct/Idv/Loan/Other columns; subaward rows observed only Contract and Grant columns. Reviewer-reported live monthly results for a Jan-Jun 2024 request returned fiscal_year='2024' with month values '4' through '9', so month grouping uses fiscal-month numbering within the fiscal year. Quarter results likewise use fiscal-quarter numbering within fiscal_year.

constraints: Do not treat results as award-level rows; the grain is one time bucket.
For group=month, interpret time_period.month as a fiscal month number within time_period.fiscal_year: 1=Oct, 2=Nov, 3=Dec, 4=Jan, 5=Feb, 6=Mar, 7=Apr, 8=May, 9=Jun, 10=Jul, 11=Aug, 12=Sep.
For group=quarter, interpret time_period.quarter as a fiscal quarter within time_period.fiscal_year: Q1=Oct-Dec, Q2=Jan-Mar, Q3=Apr-Jun, Q4=Jul-Sep.
When charting calendar month names, keep the API result order or sort by (fiscal_year, month) rather than month alone.

fields.description: Warnings and development notes returned by the live API, including date-limit and subawards deprecation guidance.

examples: For searches, time period start and end dates are currently limited to an earliest date of 2007-10-01.
The 'subawards' field will be deprecated in the future. Set 'spending_level' to 'subawards' instead.

notes: The raw MCP exposes filters as an object but does not make the nested filter semantics searchable or status-classified.
The raw profile describes aliases in prose; the semantic bundle exposes them as machine-readable acceptedValues.
This repaired semantic bundle surfaces the shared location-scope filters that the raw MCP profile still hides inside the generic filters object.

contradictions.statement: The staged contract lists only canonical group values, but source and live validation also accept cy, fy, q, and m.
contradictions.impact: Agents reading only the docs may reject valid UI-style requests; agents writing new requests should still prefer canonical group names.

quirks.statement: The live API returns a deprecation warning for the legacy subawards field and directs callers to spending_level='subawards'.
quirks.impact: New generated requests should use spending_level rather than subawards.

quirks.statement: Subaward result rows observed fewer category columns than transaction result rows.
quirks.impact: Consumers should not assume every TimeResult has Direct, Idv, Loan, or Other columns when spending_level is subawards.

quirks.statement: Month-grouped results encode time_period.month as a fiscal month number inside time_period.fiscal_year, not as a calendar month number; reviewer-reported Jan-Jun 2024 results came back as fiscal_year 2024 with month values 4 through 9.
quirks.impact: Chart builders must translate month numbers to intended fiscal or calendar labels before rendering the axis.

gaps.statement: The full AdvancedFilterObject is large; this bundle records the major filters needed for MCP discovery and request construction, not every nested search filter.
gaps.impact: Agents should use getEvidence/getDoc when constructing obscure filters not listed by listRequestFields.

risks.statement: Very broad keyword-only trend requests can scan the full indexed date range and produce long series.
risks.impact: Prefer explicit time_period and award_type_codes for repeatable analytical calls.

risks.statement: When pairing this trend with a state/county/district geography map, omitting the matching location-scope filter can produce a false same-scope comparison because the geography endpoint documents domestic defaults for those map layers.
risks.impact: Set filters.place_of_performance_scope or filters.recipient_scope explicitly so the trend and map use the same domestic-or-foreign scope.
